basic_info.name: Wenchang Tonggu Ridge Artificial Wave Pool (文昌·銅鼓嶺人工浪池) - NOT VERIFIED. No artificial wave pool exists or is under construction at Tonggu Ridge as of 2026-05. Tonggu Ridge (Tongguling 銅鼓嶺) is a National AAAA-level natural mountain-and-sea scenic area; the adjacent Moon Bay (月亮灣) and Qishui Bay (淇水灣) are NATURAL beach breaks - sometimes referred to in tourism marketing as 'Hainan's largest natural surfing destination', but with no man-made wave pool.
basic_info.location: Longlou Town, Wenchang City, Hainan (海南省文昌市龍樓鎮·銅鼓嶺景區). The wider Tonggu Ridge International Eco-Tourism Zone covers 86 km² (46 km² land + 40 km² sea), encompassing Moon Bay, Qishui Bay, Stone Park, etc., approximately 30 km from downtown Wenchang. No specific wave-pool address exists.
basic_info.operator: [uncertain] - No operator exists for any artificial wave pool. The natural Tonggu Ridge scenic area is operated by Hainan Tonggu Ridge Scenic Area management; no surf-pool operator has been announced.
basic_info.opening_date: [uncertain] - No opening date because no artificial wave pool project has been confirmed.

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: [uncertain] - No artificial wave technology brand has been linked to Tonggu Ridge. China's only operating Olympic-standard artificial wave pool (PerfectSwell SURFLAND) is in Wanning, not Wenchang.
technical_specs.pool_size: [uncertain] - No pool exists. The natural Moon Bay coastline is about 11 km long with a 5 km white-sand beach.
technical_specs.wave_height: [uncertain] - No artificial waves exist. Natural Moon Bay/Qishui Bay waves vary seasonally and are typically modest (often cited around 0.5-1.5 m for casual surfing/SUP), but this is not an artificial wave-pool spec.
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: [uncertain] - Not applicable; no artificial wave pool exists.
technical_specs.wave_types: [uncertain] - Not applicable for a wave pool. Adjacent natural Moon Bay and Qishui Bay are beach breaks.
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: [uncertain] - Not applicable; no artificial wave pool exists.

technical_specs.wave_type_classification: [uncertain] - Not applicable for an artificial pool. The adjacent natural breaks are beach breaks.

experience_guide.best_season: [uncertain] - Not applicable for an artificial pool. Natural Moon Bay/Qishui Bay surfing is more attractive in autumn-winter (northeast monsoon, October-March).
experience_guide.best_swell_window: [uncertain] - Not applicable; project is for an artificial pool. Natural swell window for Tonggu Ridge area beaches is approximately October-March.
experience_guide.crowd_level: [uncertain] - Not applicable; project does not exist. The natural scenic area itself sees moderate tourist traffic with peaks during rocket-launch viewing days at the nearby Wenchang Spaceport.

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Sea-view B&Bs in Longlou Town (e.g., Guanlange Sea-view Inn at Qishui Bay, popular for rocket-launch viewing). Resort options are limited compared to Sanya/Wanning. Price range roughly 300-1500 RMB per night.
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: [uncertain] - No on-site accommodation tied to an artificial wave pool because none exists.
surroundings.nearby_food: Local Wenchang specialty restaurants featuring Wenchang chicken, fresh seafood, and Hainanese coastal cuisine. Casual dining clusters around Longlou Town and Qishui Bay. Limited Western options.
surroundings.transport: Self-drive recommended; taxi or didi (网约车) available. Bus options from Wenchang downtown to Longlou Town. Hainan Coastal Highway (G98 island ring) provides easy access.
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: Approximately 70-90 km (~90-120 minutes) from Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK). Wenchang High-speed Rail Station is about 30-40 km away (~40-50 minutes by car). The Wenchang Commercial Spaceport is in close proximity to Tonggu Ridge.
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Tonggu Ridge + Moon Bay + Qishui Bay + Stone Park + Wenchang Spaceport (rocket launches) + Wennan Old Street + Spring Light Coconut Kingdom (春光椰子王國) form the standard Wenchang east-coast tourism circuit. No wave pool can be added to this itinerary.

reviews_reputation.pros: [uncertain] - No artificial wave-pool project exists to review. The natural Tonggu Ridge / Moon Bay area is praised for unspoiled scenery, white sand, panoramic mountaintop views, and proximity to rocket-launch viewing.
reviews_reputation.cons: [uncertain] - No artificial wave-pool project exists to review. The natural area has limited surf infrastructure (no surf schools or rental shops at scale), variable wave consistency, and remote location.
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: [uncertain] - No user reviews exist for any Tonggu Ridge artificial wave pool because none operates. Reviews of the natural scenic area on Ctrip, Mafengwo, Dianping, and Xiaohongshu are generally positive for nature/photography but rarely mention surfing facilities.
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: [uncertain] - Not applicable; project does not exist.
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: [uncertain] - No partnerships announced for any Tonggu Ridge artificial wave pool.

verification.verification_sources: Verification searches conducted 2026-05-15 returned no evidence of an artificial wave pool at Wenchang Tonggu Ridge. Sources consulted: (1) Baidu Baike entry for Hainan Tonggu Ridge Scenic Area describing it as a National AAAA natural mountain-sea scenic area with no wave-pool facility: https://baike.baidu.com/item/海南铜鼓岭景区/23603569 ; (2) Ctrip travel guides for Tonggu Ridge and Moon Bay confirming natural beach/mountain scenery only: https://gs.ctrip.com/html5/you/sight/wenchang1007/3186.html , https://gs.ctrip.com/html5/you/sight/wenchang1007/1714725.html ; (3) Haikou Bendibao Wenchang travel guide listing Tonggu Ridge as a natural attraction with no wave-pool reference: https://haikou.bendibao.com/tour/2020723/45867.shtm ; (4) Sina Travel article on Tonggu Ridge as 'internationally renowned, domestically first-class coastal resort area' (planning context only, no wave pool): https://travel.sina.com.cn/china/2009-09-01/1630104557.shtml ; (5) Tencent News article on Wenchang summer activities mentioning surfing/SUP at Qishui Bay/Moon Bay as natural water sports, not artificial pool: https://view.inews.qq.com/a/20230708A01VKF00 ; (6) Industry sources (Travel Tomorrow, TropicalHainan, WavePoolMag, Surfertoday) confirming China's only operating artificial Olympic-standard wave pool is SURFLAND in Wanning Riyue Bay (not Wenchang): https://traveltomorrow.com/china-rides-the-wave-with-its-first-purpose-built-surf-resort/ , https://www.tropicalhainan.com/chinas-first-olympic-standard-wave-pool-opens-in-wanning-hainan/ , https://wavepoolmag.com/wave-pool-china-perfectswell-hainan/ ; (7) Booking.com Tonggu Ridge sea-view B&B listings confirm rocket-launch and natural sea-view positioning, no wave pool: https://www.booking.com/hotel/cn/hai-nan-wen-chang-guan-lan-ge-qi-shui-wan-47-yi-xian-hai-jing-47-yang-tai-kan-hu.zh-cn.html. Conclusion: no artificial wave pool exists or is in construction at Tonggu Ridge as of 2026-05. The area remains a natural mountain-sea scenic destination.
